methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: A recipient's withdrawn amount must never exceed their unlocked amount, which is bounded by their total grant amount.
invariants.attack_scenario: A user bypasses the vesting schedule to withdraw tokens early, or withdraws more tokens than they were granted, draining the shared vault.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_withdrawal_bounds(address user) public view { assert(recipients[user].amountWithdrawn <= _getAmountUnlocked(recipients[user].grant)); }

invariants.description: The total cost paid by a recipient must exactly equal the cost per token multiplied by the amount of tokens withdrawn (adjusted for 1e18 precision).
invariants.attack_scenario: A user manages to withdraw tokens that require a cost payment without actually paying the required cost token amount, stealing value from the protocol.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_cost_paid_ratio(address user) public view { assert(recipients[user].costPaid == (recipients[user].amountWithdrawn / 1e18) * recipients[user].grant.costPerToken); }

invariants.description: The global total amount withdrawn must never exceed the global total amount granted minus the global total amount voided.
invariants.attack_scenario: An inflation bug or reentrancy allows the total withdrawn amount to exceed the net granted amount, leading to insolvency of the token pool.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_global_solvency() public view { assert(totalAmountWithdrawn <= totalAmountGranted - totalAmountVoided); }

invariants.description: A user's withdrawn amount must never exceed their claimed amount.
invariants.attack_scenario: A user exploits the withdrawal math to withdraw more airdrop tokens than they were allocated, draining the airdrop vault.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_airdrop_withdrawal_bounds(address user) public view { assert(withdrawnAmount[user] <= claimedAmount[user]); }

invariants.description: The calculated EIP-1559 base fee must always be strictly greater than zero to prevent free block space consumption.
invariants.attack_scenario: An attacker manipulates the gas excess to drop the base fee to zero, allowing them to spam the L2 network for free.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_basefee_minimum(uint64 _l1BlockId, uint32 _parentGasUsed) public view { assert(getBasefee(_l1BlockId, _parentGasUsed) >= 1); }
